findings.message: T005 assigns `FreeFunction` roles to `cli_composition` helpers (`tee_stderr_to_file`, `build_codex_read_only_invocation`), but the spec's CN-05/AC-06 says `cli_composition` should not have public free functions and should not allow `FreeFunction` there. That makes the new layer plan inconsistent with the accepted allowlist.

T006's final lint gate omits the new `cli` catalogue: it lists only domain/usecase/infrastructure/cli-composition/cli-driver, even though T005 adds `cli-types.json` and IN-07/AC-07 require validation of all three cli-system layers. As written, the plan can finish without ever linting the new `cli` layer.

`ErrorType` is inconsistent across the artifacts: `spec.json:288` says the lint allowlist should be only `cli` + `cli_composition`, but this task broadens it to `domain/usecase/infrastructure/cli/cli_composition` (and the S3 summary at `impl-plan.json:75` says the rule only adds `cli` + `cli_composition`). As written, the plan cannot satisfy both the spec and the task description.

AC-03 requires an ErrorType KindLayerConstraint with permitted layers only cli and cli_composition, and T003 repeats that rule. Because KindLayerConstraint is role-global, this would also forbid ErrorType in the existing domain/usecase/infrastructure layers, contradicting the plan's R1 update that preserves ErrorType's existing layer-flexible placements and only adds cli and cli_composition while excluding cli_driver. The lint rule needs to include the existing permitted layers as well, or it will make the plan internally inconsistent and cause catalogue linting to reject valid existing ErrorType entries.

OS-05 says per-type role assignment work such as declaring `*Args`/`*Command` structs as `Dto` is out of scope and will be finalized by a later implementation track, but the same spec makes catalogue authoring in scope in IN-07/AC-06 and T005 requires this track to declare each existing public CLI-layer type with an allowlisted role. That leaves implementers with the same work both required and excluded, so the plan is semantically inconsistent until OS-05 is narrowed or the catalogue-authoring tasks are moved out of scope.

AC-03 defines the global `Dto` `KindLayerConstraint` permitted layers as only `infrastructure`, `cli`, and `cli_driver`. That conflicts with the existing R1 convention/usecase-output-DTO design that allows justified usecase DTOs, and it also conflicts with T004's framing that the existing `Dto` row should only gain CLI-layer columns. If implemented literally, the new global lint rule would make legitimate usecase DTO catalogue entries illegal, so the permitted layer list needs to preserve the existing usecase allowance or the plan must explicitly update the convention and downstream assumptions.

T005 requires `cli_composition::CommandOutcome` to be listed in the `cli-composition-types.json` catalogue, but the spec's allowlist for `cli_composition` only permits `CompositionRoot` and `ErrorType` (see `IN-04` / `AC-06`). Since `CommandOutcome` is a DTO-style data carrier in the same task narrative, following T005 would force a `Dto` entry into a layer where the plan later requires `sotp track lint` to pass with zero allowlist violations. This makes the catalogue-authoring and lint-gate tasks mutually unsatisfiable unless the plan either removes/privatizes that public type before cataloguing or explicitly changes the `cli_composition` allowlist.

T002 is ordered before the cli/cli_composition/cli_driver catalogues and rustdoc baselines are created, but it still requires a full `cargo make ci` immediately after enabling TDDD and adding `catalogue_file`/`schema_export` blocks. Those dependent artifacts are explicitly deferred to T005/T006, so the plan asks implementers to reach a standalone CI-green state at a point where the TDDD-enabled layers are not yet fully materialized. Narrow T002 to the layer flip verification, or move the catalogue/baseline work before the full CI gate.

`T005`'s `cli_driver` catalogue only assigns `Dto` and `PrimaryAdapter` roles, but `CN-05` and `AC-06` require `cli_driver` to admit `FreeFunction` as well and explicitly call out public `render_*` functions. Without cataloguing those functions, the plan cannot satisfy the declared allowlist for that layer, so the T006 lint gate is not achievable as written.

OS-03 rejects an alternative that "does not add `PrimaryAdapter`" while also describing that same alternative as a single `PrimaryAdapter` role covering wire and invoke/render. This contradicts the rest of the spec, which consistently requires adding `PrimaryAdapter` and `CompositionRoot` as two roles, and it also contradicts the referenced ADR's rejected alternative, which is not adding `CompositionRoot` and letting `PrimaryAdapter` cover both responsibilities. Update the first role name in OS-03 to `CompositionRoot` so the rejected alternative is semantically coherent.

T006 says `sotp track lint` should cover “all five active catalogues” and that infrastructure is absent until a later restore, but this same plan-artifacts scope already includes `infrastructure-types.json` for the `infrastructure` layer. Treating infrastructure as absent contradicts the Phase 2 artifact set and can make the implementation skip lint/coverage for an enabled catalogue rather than validating the empty infrastructure catalogue that was generated for this track.

CN-03 constrains the lint-rule implementation to `.harness/catalogue-lint/config.json` “only”, while AC-03 and T003 allow or directly require updating the corresponding preset file. These are conflicting instructions for the same T003 work item, so an implementer following the constraint could reject the preset update that the task/acceptance criteria require.

AC-04's `NoRoleInMethodSignature` list does not match the stated rule in CN-02 that `PrimaryAdapter` must keep domain/usecase roles out of public signatures. The catalogue declares additional domain/usecase `RoleKind` variants such as `DomainService`, `Specification`, `Factory`, `DomainEvent`, `EventPolicy`, and `SpecificationPort`, but they are absent from the forbidden list, so implementing this plan would still allow those inner roles to leak through `PrimaryAdapter` method signatures.

T003 promises to extend the `FreeFunction` `KindLayerConstraint`, but the type plan for `RoleKind` only covers DataRole + ContractRole variants and adds no FunctionRole/FreeFunction discriminant or conversion path. Because the lint rule targets are expressed through `RoleKind`, this plan cannot actually enforce the AC-03 FreeFunction layer constraint; `sotp track lint` would still have no role target for FreeFunction entries. Add FunctionRole support to the RoleKind/linter migration or remove the FreeFunction enforcement claim.

T005's cli catalogue instructions list only `CliError` and public free functions for `apps/cli`, but AC-06 requires each layer's existing public types to be catalogued under the layer allowlist, and the ADR/spec identify cli `Dto` entries as part of that allowlist. As written, implementers can complete T005 while omitting the cli clap/input DTO types entirely, leaving the cli layer catalogue incomplete against the acceptance criteria. Include the cli DTO/public command or args types in T005 or explicitly justify why none are public/cataloguable.

`DataRole`'s catalogue text says all six public constructor/accessor methods have match arms that must be extended for `CompositionRoot` and `PrimaryAdapter`, but five of those methods are simple constructors (`value_object`, `entity`, `aggregate_root`, `domain_service`, `use_case`) with no match arms and no relationship to the new unit variants. Only `variant_name`/string conversion and exhaustive consumers need variant arms. Leaving this wording in the authoritative type catalogue can send T001 implementers toward unnecessary or nonsensical constructor changes instead of the actual migration surface.

`T005` still instructs creation of the deleted hyphenated artifacts (`cli-composition-types.json` / `cli-driver-types.json`) instead of the canonical underscore-named files, so the plan is pointing at the wrong artifact set. The same mismatch is repeated in [impl-plan.json](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core-002/track/items/cli-layers-tddd-and-role-placement-lint-2026-06-24/impl-plan.json#L29).

T006 says the infrastructure catalogue is expected to remain empty, but this plan bundle also adds `CodexPlannerAdapter` to `infrastructure-types.json` and renders that node in the contract map. The acceptance criterion is therefore internally inconsistent.

`plan.md` still describes the `cli_driver` catalogue as containing public `render_*` helpers from `apps/cli-driver/src/render.rs`, but the rendered artefacts only surface one `FreeFunction`: `cli_driver::ref_verify::format_pair_status` in the `ref_verify` module. That makes the phase plan and the generated catalogue disagree about where the free-function boundary actually is, so later reviewers will be looking in the wrong place.

`PlannerPortError` and the surrounding usecase contract are still mechanism-specific: the port docs say it executes a subprocess, and the error enum exposes `BriefingFileNotFound` and `Subprocess(String)` instead of planner-level failures. That leaks infrastructure detail across the usecase boundary, so the adapter will have to surface subprocess/path wording to higher layers. Recast these as conceptual planner errors and keep process/file details inside the infrastructure adapter.

`PlanCompositionRoot` is marked `modify` here, but the track plan says newly declared types in this round must use `add`, and it explicitly treats the plan composition root as a new addition. That makes the catalogue state inconsistent with the rest of the plan-artifacts set.

`spec.json` / `spec.md` still refer to `cli-composition-types.json` and `cli-driver-types.json` in IN-07 and AC-06, but the rest of this plan-artifacts bundle uses the underscore filenames `cli_composition-types.json` and `cli_driver-types.json`. That makes the acceptance criterion point at the wrong catalog files and breaks the cross-document reference.

The rendered catalogue view drops the `action: add` marker for newly introduced entries: `PlanCompositionRoot` is shown with action `—` here even though `cli_composition-types.json` declares it as `add`, and the same mismatch appears for `PlanInput`/`PlanDriver`, `PlannerPort`/`PlanRunOutput`/`PlannerPortError`, and `CodexPlannerAdapter` in the other rendered type views. This contradicts the catalogue source and the plan's rule that newly declared types use `action: add`, making the plan artifacts misstate which entries are new.

`cli_composition` is still rendered with DTO and FreeFunction catalogue rows (`CommandOutcome`, `build_codex_read_only_invocation`, `tee_stderr_to_file`) even though the spec/plan define the layer allowlist as only `CompositionRoot` and `ErrorType` and explicitly say no DTO or FreeFunction entries are declared for this layer. If these delete tombstones are intended, the plan contract needs to qualify that it means non-delete entries only; otherwise the catalogue/view contradicts the D3 allowlist it claims to satisfy.

The plan says `PrimaryAdapter` holds an injected use case and that `SecondaryPort` must not be mixed into the cli-system catalogues, but the new `PlanDriver` primary adapter is catalogued with a `planner: Arc<dyn usecase::planner::PlannerPort>` field. That makes the thin-bin plan contradict its own role-placement semantics and bypasses the promised separation between primary adapters and usecase/secondary-port roles.

`cli` catalogue entries are supposed to cover public command/args DTOs and public dispatch free functions, while private helpers are explicitly not catalogued. These live `FreeFunction` additions describe `plan_input_from_args` and `review_input_from_args` as “private helper” functions, so the catalogue is blessing private test helpers as current cli-layer free-function surface contrary to the plan’s own public-surface rule.

`IN-08` still specifies a direct `PlanDriver -> PlannerPort` wiring, but `IN-09` requires the final state to be `PlanDriver -> PlannerService -> PlannerInteractor -> PlannerPort`. The same mismatch is repeated later in `AC-11` vs `AC-12`, so the spec currently asks for two incompatible planner topologies.

AC-12 says the `*Service` trait_impls live under infrastructure `SecondaryAdapter` entries, but the actual catalogue places those implementations on the `*Interactor` types in `libs/usecase` (`ArchInteractor`, `ConventionsInteractor`, `FileInteractor`, `PlannerInteractor`, `VerifyInteractor`). That makes the acceptance criterion self-contradictory and points implementers at the wrong layer.

`VerifyCompositionRoot` still exposes `verify_hooks_path(project_root) -> Result<CommandOutcome, CompositionError>` under the `CompositionRoot` role. ADR 1328 D2 says composition roots are wire-only and must not invoke use cases, so this leaves a command entry point in a type that is supposed to be pure composition and duplicates `VerifyDriver`'s responsibility.

`S6` attributes the "keep the diff under 500 lines" rationale to `CN-04`, but `CN-04` in this scope is the `CompositionRoot`/`PrimaryAdapter` placement rule, not a size-budget constraint. This mislabels the constraint in both `plan.md` and the mirrored `impl-plan.json` text, so readers will look at the wrong rule when checking the task split.

T013 lists only T012 as its dependency, but the S6 section says all T008-T013 tasks depend on T007, and T013 is the task that introduces the PlanDriver PrimaryAdapter signature using `Arc<dyn PlannerService>`. As written, the dependency graph permits T013 before the lint-config correction that allows `ApplicationService` in PrimaryAdapter signatures, contradicting the section-level ordering and making that path fail the stated lint gate. Add T007 to T013's dependency text or clarify the ordering consistently.

IN-09 describes `PlanDriver` as one of the current five `cli_driver` primary adapters already holding `Arc<dyn PlannerPort>`, but the same plan set treats `PlanDriver`/`PlanInput` as new additions to be introduced in T013 and the cli_driver catalogue marks `PlanDriver` as `action: add`. That makes the audit finding and implementation scope disagree: the four existing drivers need modification, while the Plan pair is creation work. Rephrase the Plan pair as planned/add work or align the catalogue/task status with the audit claim.

T007 says AC-04 will be satisfied by removing only `ApplicationService` from the PrimaryAdapter forbidden-role list, but T003 records the current rule as also containing `DomainService`, `Specification`, `Factory`, `DomainEvent`, `EventPolicy`, and `SpecificationPort`, while AC-04/IN-03 require the shorter exact list. Following T007 as written leaves those extra roles in place, so the plan does not actually reach its own AC-04 rule shape.

AC-12 says `PlanDriver` changes its field from `Arc<dyn PlannerPort>` to `Arc<dyn PlannerService>`, but IN-09 says `PlanDriver`/`PlanInput` do not yet exist and the new `PlanDriver` is introduced from the start with `Arc<dyn PlannerService>`; T013 says the same. That makes AC-12's PlanDriver evidence impossible to satisfy as a real before/after change and contradicts the rest of the plan.

ADR D3's lint-rule block omits `usecase` from the `Dto` `permitted_layers`, but the generated spec's AC-03 requires `Dto` to be permitted in `usecase` as well as `infrastructure`, `cli`, and `cli_driver`. Both passages describe the lint config to be implemented, so this leaves an executor with conflicting role-placement requirements for existing usecase DTOs.

`IN-03` now includes the exact `NoRoleInMethodSignature` forbidden-role list and the `ApplicationService` exception, but task coverage still maps it only to `T001`, which only adds the `PrimaryAdapter` role machinery. The task that actually normalizes that rule is `T007`, so the coverage artifact contradicts the spec/plan and can mark IN-03 covered without scheduling the required lint-config fix.

S6 states that every task in the section, including T012, depends on T007, but the T012 task description later says it depends only on T001. That gives two conflicting execution orders for the planner usecase/infrastructure work: either T012 is blocked behind the lint-config fix, or it can proceed once DataRole/RoleKind exist. The plan should make this dependency explicit one way or the other so implementers do not follow different task ordering.

T008 is marked done even though this section says it depends on T007, and T007 is still unchecked in the same plan. That makes the task order internally inconsistent: a downstream task is recorded as resolved before its declared prerequisite.

T010 is still marked `todo` here, but the regenerated signal docs in `usecase-types.md` and `cli_driver-types.md` already show the File stack as implemented (`FileService`, `FileInteractor`, and `FileDriver` are all 🔵). That leaves the plan artifacts internally inconsistent: the task list says the work is unfinished while the layer signals say it is present. Either mark T010 done with its commit hash, or keep the signals yellow until the source change is actually part of this branch.

`S6` says the Arc<dyn *Service> adapter tasks depend on `T007`, but the plan already marks `T009` and `T010` done while `T007` is still todo. That makes the recorded state internally inconsistent: either the dependency is wrong or the completion order is. The same mismatch is reflected in `impl-plan.json` where `T007` is todo and `T009`/`T010` are done.

`T012` is still rendered as unchecked/todo even though the related type catalogue now marks `PlannerService` and `PlannerInteractor` as implemented (`🔵`). These artifacts now give conflicting planning signals: the task that is supposed to add those Planner usecase roles is still presented as not done, while the type view says those roles are already present. Update the task state or the regenerated type signals so the plan has one coherent status for the Planner usecase work.

The `plan_input_from_args` entry was downgraded from 🔵 to 🟡, but this plan-artifact refresh is supposed to reflect only the four intended signal flips (PlanDriver, PlanCompositionRoot, VerifyDriver, VerifyCompositionRoot). That leaves an extra unaccounted signal change in the generated catalogued artifacts.

`tee_stderr_to_file` now lives in `apps/cli`, so the bin layer itself owns the Codex subprocess stderr tee and log-file write. That is direct application I/O outside the driver/composition boundary; this helper should stay behind `cli_composition`/`infrastructure` instead of being implemented in the CLI command module.

`build_prompt` still performs a filesystem probe with `Path::is_file()` in bin-layer production code. That keeps application I/O in `apps/cli` instead of flowing through the driver/composition boundary, which violates the thin-bin contract for this scope.

`execute_codex_local` now builds the prompt with `resolve_prompt` instead of the validated `build_prompt` path, so production no longer checks that `--briefing-file` exists before launching the planner. A typoed briefing path will now proceed into Codex and fail later, which regresses the CLI's early validation behavior.

Removing `apps/cli/src/commands/plan/tests.rs` leaves the public `PlanCommand::execute` / `execute_codex_local` path with no CLI-layer coverage in this scope. The new wrapper still has user-visible branching (`--briefing-file` vs `--prompt`, file-existence validation) plus driver dispatch, so a regression here can now slip through undetected.

`ReviewCommand::CodexLocal` no longer has any tests that exercise `execute_codex_local` itself. `review/tests.rs` still covers helpers, but not the `CodexLocalArgs -> ReviewInput::RunCodex -> outcome emission` path, so regressions in the new thin dispatch would not be caught.

The `cli` layer’s `schema_export.notes` does not include the required explicit hint that T006 routes bin targets through `libs/infrastructure/src/schema_export/bin_target.rs`. It only says nightly rustdoc supports bin targets, so the architecture SSoT is still missing the documented handoff to the bin-target export path.

`bin/sotp review local --round-type final --group other --track-id cli-layers-tddd-and-role-placement-lint-2026-06-24 --briefing-file tmp/reviewer-runtime/briefing-other.md` could not complete in this read-only workspace because the reviewer failed to initialize `output-last-message` (`Read-only file system (os error 30)`). As a result, the required final round was not recorded; `bin/sotp review results` only shows the previously recorded fast zero_findings entry.

The usecase port signature exposes the adapter's filesystem/session-log concern by requiring callers to pass a concrete `PathBuf` runtime directory. The briefing calls out file paths as infrastructure details that must not appear in this SecondaryPort signature, and the usecase purity rules keep runtime/file effects at the CLI or infrastructure edge. Make the port contract use application-level DTO data only, or keep session-log path selection entirely inside the infrastructure adapter/composition wiring.

`PlannerPortError::Subprocess(String)` makes the usecase boundary expose the infrastructure mechanism and raw adapter error text instead of a usecase-level planner failure concept. That breaks the error abstraction described by the usecase severity policy: callers now need to know that the planner is a subprocess and may receive subprocess-shaped details. Map these failures to conceptual variants such as planner unavailable/timed out/failed without leaking the underlying I/O/process wording.

`PlannerInteractor::run_codex_local` probes the filesystem with `path.is_file()` before delegating. That makes the usecase layer reach into runtime state directly instead of staying a pure orchestrator, and it collapses path/permission failures into `MissingPromptSource` rather than keeping them behind a port or adapter.

`PlannerUnavailable` carries an arbitrary `String` and `Display` prints it verbatim. The infrastructure adapter populates that field with raw process/OS error text, so any caller that surfaces `PlannerPortError` will leak internal host/path/process details across the usecase boundary. Replace this with a sanitized usecase-level variant or keep the raw cause out of the public display text.

This capability reference is now stale: it still says `role` must be one of the 15 type-section values and that `RoleKind` covers `15 + 4 = 19` variants, but the updated kind-selection convention in `knowledge/conventions/type-designer-kind-selection.md` now defines 17 `DataRole` variants. The harness surface now has two incompatible SSoTs for the same catalogue schema, so agents following this doc will continue to reject `CompositionRoot` / `PrimaryAdapter` updates.

`resolve_bin_target_name` silently returns the first `kind == "bin"` target from `cargo metadata`, and it ignores `default_run` and the presence of multiple `[[bin]]` entries. For any package with more than one binary target, this can export the wrong rustdoc JSON depending on metadata ordering instead of failing closed or selecting the canonical binary explicitly.

`resolve_target_dir` accepts a relative `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` and blindly joins it to `workspace_root`. A value like `../outside` escapes the trusted root, so the rustdoc adapter can operate outside the workspace without the containment check used elsewhere (`is_safe_briefing_path` / CN-04 fail-closed).

Timeout handling can deadlock here: the code kills only the direct child, then always `join()`s the stderr tee thread. If Codex spawns descendants that keep the stderr pipe open, `join()` never returns; and if the child exits between `try_wait()` and `kill()`, the `?` turns a timeout into a spurious `PlannerUnavailable`. The established reviewer/dry-check adapters avoid both failure modes by ignoring the kill race and not joining drain threads on timeout.

This target-dir guard is only lexical; it never rejects symlinked roots before `parse_rustdoc_json()` later calls `read_to_string()` on the resolved path. A symlinked `workspace_root` or `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` can therefore redirect the read outside the trusted workspace, which conflicts with the repo's existing symlink-rejection boundary pattern.

P1: On the timeout path this suppresses `child.kill()` failures and then always calls `child.wait()`. If the child cannot be terminated (or the kill race loses in a way that leaves it running), `wait()` can block indefinitely, so the adapter no longer fails closed on timeout. The previous code surfaced `PlannerUnavailable` immediately instead of hanging.

P1: This now drops any function whose rustdoc path has only one segment. The existing baseline renderer already treats `[item_name]` as a valid degenerate path, so root-level free functions can exist; with this guard they disappear from Phase 2 entirely instead of being evaluated and reported. That silently omits legitimate items from the signal report.

`prepare_session_log_path()` only combines the PID with a millisecond timestamp, so two `run()` calls in the same millisecond can generate the same log filename and `File::create` will truncate the earlier session log. The existing collision-safe pattern in [codex_common.rs](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core-002/libs/infrastructure/src/codex_common.rs) uses an atomic counter for exactly this reason.

The bin-target path lookup uses `format!("{bin_name}.json")` verbatim, unlike the lib path which normalizes `-` to `_`. A valid `[[bin]]` target with a hyphenated name will look for a nonexistent artifact and fail even though `cargo rustdoc --bin` succeeded.

`build_function_identity_map` now strips the first path segment for every function (`skip(1)`), so a lib function like `cli::module::compute` collapses to `module::compute` just like a bin target. That makes the canonical function identity non-canonical and will mis-key or duplicate function signals unless every downstream caller has been updated to the same shortened form. If the goal was only to normalize `[[bin]]` targets, gate this on target kind instead of applying it unconditionally.

`load_catalogue_spec_signals_for_view` rejects a symlinked leaf, but it still calls `read_to_string` on the path after only checking `symlink_metadata()` for the final component. If the track directory or any ancestor is a symlink, the loader will follow it and can read a file outside the intended track root; this should use the repo's full symlink-rejection pattern (`reject_symlinks_below`/trusted-root validation) instead of leaf-only checking.

`allow_outside_workspace` is derived from `configured_dir.is_absolute()`, but the `cargo metadata target_directory` path passed into this helper is already absolutized by Cargo. That means a workspace config such as `target-dir = "../outside"` can arrive here as an absolute path outside the trusted root and be accepted, bypassing the intended CN-04 fail-closed relative-escape rejection. Preserve the original source/origin or keep metadata-derived paths confined unless they came from an explicitly absolute `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`.

`RoleKind` now admits `FunctionRole` variants, but `NoRoleInMethodSignature` still only scans type and trait entries. That means a config like `forbidden_roles: [FreeFunction]` or `UseCaseFunction` parses successfully and then becomes a silent no-op instead of being rejected or enforced.

Adding `FunctionRole` to `RoleKind::ALL` makes `RuleTarget` able to name `FreeFunction`/`UseCaseFunction`, but only `KindLayerConstraint` was wired to inspect `catalogue.functions`. The other rule kinds still iterate only `type_entries_for_target`/`trait_entries_for_target`, so a function-only target now constructs successfully and then silently no-ops instead of being rejected. Either validate unsupported function-role targets up front or make the other branches function-aware.

The new `KindLayerConstraint` function-entry path is not exercised by any test, and `RoleKind::from_function_role` has no unit coverage. If the `FunctionPath` filtering or role mapping regresses, CI will still pass because nothing asserts that a forbidden-layer `FunctionEntry` produces a violation.
